chapter_context.summary: Jehu exterminates all seventy sons of Ahab, slays Ahaziah's relatives, and executes a massive slaughter of Baal's worshippers to destroy their temple; he maintains the golden calves.

outline.text: Jehu Orders the Slaughter of Ahab's Seventy Sons in Samaria
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehu Orders the Slaughter of Ahab's Seventy Sons in Samaria (10:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehu Slays Forty-Two Relatives of Ahaziah of Judah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehu Slays Forty-Two Relatives of Ahaziah of Judah (10:12-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehu and Jehonadab Ride Together; Ahab's Remaining House Destroyed
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehu and Jehonadab Ride Together; Ahab's Remaining House Destroyed (10:15-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehu Gathers Worshippers of Baal and Massacres Them; Destroys Temple
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehu Gathers Worshippers of Baal and Massacres Them; Destroys Temple (10:18-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehu's Partial Obedience; Keeps Golden Calves; Death of Jehu
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehu's Partial Obedience; Keeps Golden Calves; Death of Jehu (10:29-36), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 10 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
